changes.before: The others had looked patient, which was more dangerous.
changes.after: The others had worn patient faces, which was more dangerous.

changes.before: She heard herself doing it again: careful, pared down, built for someone she had already decided would need the softer version.
changes.after: There it was again: careful, pared down, built for someone she had already decided would need the softer version.

changes.before: Sylvanas's gaze did not soften, but it sharpened.
Even the wards seemed to tighten, the blue lines along the wall burning a shade brighter.
changes.after: The blue lines along the wall burned a shade brighter.

changes.before: "Yes," Jaina said, and this time she heard the restraint in her own voice.
changes.after: "Yes," Jaina said, and this time the restraint was in her own voice.

changes.before: the clean ward-scent stripped away so something metallic and wet could breathe underneath
changes.after: the clean ward-scent stripped away and metal-wet stone left underneath

changes.before: resonance snapping through her palm like a numb limb waking
changes.after: resonance snapping through her palm and breaking the numbness there

changes.before: the land itself had stopped being only a carrier of signs and become part of the mechanism
changes.after: the land had stopped merely carrying signs of it and started feeding it

changes.before: She heard it coming and despised it. "No—that is not the useful answer."
changes.after: She cut it off. "No—that's the smaller answer."

changes.before: Jaina cut herself off halfway through the explanation she had been about to offer with her hands and a patient, simplifying tone, because Sylvanas had already done the work on the map.
changes.after: Jaina bit off the explanation she had been about to soften for her, because Sylvanas had already followed the convergence south.

changes.before: "Yes," Jaina said, letting herself speak in her own register at last.
wolves and hooves and the copper taste in her mouth crowding out every elegant thought she had ever had
changes.after: wolves and hooves and the copper taste in her mouth crowding out thought

changes.before: making a living wall where Jaina's left flank had been open a breath earlier
from something common enough that deer and wolves could carry the same lesson
changes.after: from whatever deer and wolves touched alike

changes.before: not because Sylvanas had dismissed her but because she had not
changes.after: Sylvanas had taken the diagnosis for what it was

changes.before: the road ahead already belonging to Sylvanas's decision
changes.after: the road ahead in Sylvanas's hands

changes.before: this time when she looked at Sylvanas she did not see a soldier she needed to drag toward the truth. She saw the officer whose read of the road had already kept her alive twice
changes.after: Sylvanas was no longer a soldier she needed to drag toward the truth, but the officer whose read of the road had already kept her alive twice

observations.finding: Softened visual reporting by swapping 'looked patient' for the tighter 'worn patient faces.'
Cut the vague 'something metallic and wet could breathe underneath' image back to a concrete smell.
Compressed the staff-resonance beat without losing Jaina's bodily read of the magic.
Trimmed the escort-protection sentence to keep the tactical realization from going abstract.
Removed the ornamental 'elegant thought' flourish so the wolf hit lands in pure panic.
Removed 'She heard herself' so Jaina catches her own habit directly instead of through a filter layer.
Collapsed 'did not soften, but sharpened' to the committed verb.
Cut the wards 'seeming to tighten' and kept the brightening runes as the concrete pressure cue.
Swapped 'looked back' for 'turned back' to keep Sylvanas physical and decisive.
Removed another self-hearing construction from Jaina's internal calibration.
Took 'certain it would get this far' off the contamination and grounded it in depth and reach instead.
Replaced 'become part of the mechanism' with a grounded cause-and-effect image: the land feeding it.
Removed self-conscious commentary about the 'useful answer' and kept the sharper correction.
Cut explanatory prose about simplifying tone; kept the actual relational beat that Sylvanas already understood.
Removed the banned noun 'register' from narration and recast it as Jaina stopping her self-translation.
Dropped an unnecessary breath-based timing marker from the ranger coverage beat.
Reduced one 'something' construction in the contamination diagnosis.
Shortened a not-because/but-because thought into a clean statement of trust and division of labor.
Grounded the escort spacing in Sylvanas's hands instead of her 'decision.'
Removed the did-not-see/she-saw chain from the closing trust beat.
Sylvanas's opener stayed clean: hard command phrasing, no memo-language contamination.
Jaina's 'permission slips' line remained plainspoken enough for a frustrated field argument.
Sylvanas's terms block reads as field command rather than bureaucratic briefing, so it stayed.
Jaina's roadside diagnosis remains concrete: soil, water, things growing here. No clinical spillover.
Quarantine and cordon stayed because this is emergency field language inside a plague scene, not fake-smart document voice.

flags: Polish pass ran on an already style-audited chapter; safe compression topped out at 105 words without flattening Jaina's POV or the mounted-action rhythm.
Kept 'Almost a smile.' on line 99; it reads as a character beat, not expression-decomposition sludge.
